  [ok] SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT player_api_id) AS cnt
FROM Player_Attributes
WHERE crossing = (SELECT MAX(crossing) FROM Player_Attributes)
  AND preferred_foot = 'left';
FINAL ANSWER: 2 players.

SQL:
```
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT player_api_id) AS cnt
FROM Player_Attributes
WHERE crossing = (SELECT MAX(crossing) FROM Player_Attributes)
  AND preferred_foot = 'left';
```

SQL ATTEMPTS:
FINAL ANSWER: I can build the query, but one quick clarification before I hand this to the SQLWriter:

- Do you mean:
  1) Include all molecules that have at least one single bond (i.e. any bond with bond_type = '-'), or
  2) Include only molecules whose every bond is single (i.e. all bonds for that molecule have bond_type = '-')?

While you decide, here are the exact schema details I will use and the observed value formats (so filters match the stored values exactly):

- Tables to use:
  - bond
    - bond.bond_type (TEXT) — sample values: '-' , '='
    - bond.molecule_id (TEXT) — FK to molecule.molecule_id
  - atom
    - atom.element (TEXT) — sample values: 'o' for oxygen (lowercase), 'c', 'cl', 'h', ...
    - atom.molecule_id (TEXT) — FK to molecule.molecule_id
  - molecule
    - molecule.molecule_id
